rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND CRONIN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present admissible evidence that Mr. Cronin was exposed to asbestos attributable to defendants. Defendant¿s objections to paragraphs 43-47 of Mr. Cohen¿s declaration are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL R BARNES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 15, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 2. DEFENDANT DOWMAN PRODUCTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the sole issue of punitive damages. Considered together, Mr. Paul Barnes¿ deposition testimony, Mr. Lewis¿deposition and trial testimony, Mr. Cook¿s declaration, Mr. Cook¿s declaration, Mr. Wellington¿s deposition and trial testimony and Mr. Dowds¿ deposition testimony create a triable issue whether Mr. Barnes was exposed to asbestos-containing FixAll manufactured by defendant. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression necessary to warrant an award of punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH MCGREW VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 15, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 6. DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendant Union Carbide¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-4 and 6-10 (sophisticated intermediary and bulk supplier defenses and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 5 (false representation). The sophisticated intermediary defense does not shield defendant from liability. (Stewart). Defendant failed to present evidence showing what knowledge Hamilton Materials had about the hazards of asbestos and what warnings, if any, it provided to Hamilton Materials. The bulk supplier defense is not available to a supplier of raw asbestos in asbestos exposure cases because asbestos is inherently dangerous and raw asbestos fibers do not change when they become a component of an asbestos-containing product. (Garza; Jenkins; Arena). As to punitive damages, there are triable issues whether defendant made misrepresentations about and/or withheld material information concerning its knowledge of the hazards of asbestos and thus whether it acted in conscious disregard for the safety of persons who were exposed to its asbestos. Plaintiffs dismissed their cause of action for false representation. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH MCGREW VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 15, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 6, DEFENDANT EXXON MOBIL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. The declaration of Robert Mingram creates a triable issue whether Mr. McGrew was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation by the Humble Oil employees. The objections to Mr. Mingram¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)
